name: Technology Credit Union
description_short: Technology Credit Union is a member-owned credit union offering personal and business banking, loans, investment services, and wealth management across multiple states.
description_long: Technology Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union (ABA Routing #121181976) that operates as a member-owned financial cooperative. The organization serves individuals, businesses, and non-profit organizations through a multi-state presence, with specific operations noted in Idaho, Texas, and California. The credit union has established itself as a comprehensive financial services provider with institutional depth, including a dedicated wealth management division and employer partnership program. TechCU offers a broad suite of consumer banking products including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, mortgage loans, home equity lines of credit, personal loans, and Visa credit cards with rewards programs. The organization provides business banking services including business checking/savings accounts, commercial lending, certificates, and money market accounts. Additional offerings include digital banking platforms, solar loans, student loans, financial education resources, and a "Tech Car Shopper" service. Investment and wealth management services include private banking, financial planning, estate planning, IRA rollovers, stock option planning, and guided wealth portfolios. TechCU distinguishes itself through specialized services including an employer partnership program (Tech CU@Work) offering financial benefits to employees, a dedicated wealth management division with private banking services, and solar-specific lending products (Solar $mart loans). The organization maintains multiple digital channels including a virtual branch and dedicated business digital banking platform. They offer relationship rewards programs and member-only perks tied to their Sharks credit card (advertised at 5.65% APR). The credit union provides free credit score access and comprehensive financial education resources. As a credit union, TechCU operates on a non-profit, member-owned model theoretically offering better rates and lower fees than traditional banks. However, the website provides limited rate transparency, specific fee schedules, or membership eligibility criteria. The organization's service area appears regionally focused, which may limit accessibility for non-residents. While the breadth of offerings is extensive, actual product competitiveness and member satisfaction metrics are not disclosed on the public-facing website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Comprehensive financial services: checking, savings, auto/mortgage/personal loans, business banking, investment management, and wealth services in one institution
Credit union structure: member-owned, non-profit model typically offering better rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Specialized solar lending products (Solar $mart) and solar loans for renewable energy customers
Employer partnership program (Tech CU@Work) providing financial benefits access to employees of partner companies
Private banking and wealth management division with estate planning, IRA rollovers, and stock option planning services
Digital banking infrastructure including virtual branch and dedicated business digital banking platform
Relationship rewards program and credit card rewards (Sharks card at 5.65% APR with member-only perks)
Free credit score access and financial education resources including calculators and blog
cons: Limited rate transparency: specific APRs, APYs, and fee schedules not disclosed on website, requiring member inquiry
Unclear membership eligibility criteria: no explicit explanation of who can join or any geographic/employment restrictions listed
Regional service footprint: operations in Idaho, Texas, and California may limit accessibility for residents outside these states
No online account opening visible: website directs users to 'Join Now' without clear self-service onboarding process
Limited product detail: loan terms, credit card benefits, and account features require navigation to separate pages or contact
best_for: Technology industry employees in Idaho, Texas, and California seeking employer-affiliated financial benefits
Small business owners requiring comprehensive commercial banking, lending, and financial planning services
Homeowners in sunny markets interested in solar financing options combined with traditional mortgage/HELOC services
Affluent individuals seeking wealth management, private banking, and estate planning from a member-owned institution
diagnosis: Technology Credit Union is best suited for individuals and small business owners in its service areas (Idaho, Texas, California) seeking comprehensive banking and investment services through a member-owned, non-profit institution. The main caveat is lack of public rate transparency and unclear membership eligibility—prospective members must contact the organization directly to compare actual APRs, fees, and confirm they meet membership criteria.
services: Mortgage loans and home purchase financing
Home equity loans and lines of credit (HELOCs)
Visa credit cards with rewards (Sharks card)
Commercial lending and small business loans
Certificates of deposit and business money market accounts
Financial planning and estate planning services
Investment management and stock option planning
Virtual branch and digital banking platforms
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 2010 N First St, San Jose, CA 95131
meta_description: Member-owned credit union offering personal and business banking, loans, investment services, and wealth management across multiple states.
